# Transcrição: Rapid Fire Testing

- **Fonte:** Rapid Fire Testing
- **Duração:** 08:13

## [00:00 - 03:01]

Okay, so if you're unsure on what service offering you should be leading with or what persona to go after, so really nailing down your niche, one of the strategies or little hack that you can use is to roll out what we call a rapid fire test. Now we go into this extensively inside of FATC, but I'm going to show you guys how to set this up really quickly because you're not really doing a full full test here. All you're really going to do is essentially the first round, which is understanding who the persona is and we can flip that around and also use the same strategy if it's different service offerings that you have. So over here I started a sheet, as you can see here, let me just move this across over here, there we go, for my rapid fire testing and this is basically mapping out my different personas. So the example given here is a service offering of Facebook group growth campaigns. So let's say that's a service that you offer or that's something that you're very strong at, at helping people grow their Facebook groups. Well then you can ask yourself, who are the different types of people that would need the solution, right? So first of all, really nail down what is the core problem and also what is the core human desire attached to this. So the core problem or the pain point of why someone may be wanting to grow a group is they're not getting enough revenue through their business, their funnels aren't converting very well, they need to be a lot more effective at diversifying their traffic channels, they want to compound the results from their traffic, they want to build their own audience. And from that, ask yourself, who are the people that have these, these problems that I can solve with the service offering. And so this is just an example, but here we're going, okay, let's say it could be coaches or it could be E-com brands. Now obviously there would be so many more with, with just the service offering and you really want to flush this out. If you already know who your persona is and you want to actually test out different service offerings, you could just flip this whole thing on its head. So instead of this being persona one and persona two, just go service offering one, service offering two, it's the same concept. Now I put together some copy here. What I would recommend is that you have at least three pieces of copy. Now what we're going to do is we're going to go inside of Facebook ads. Let me move this over, over here so we can see this. And we're going to create a campaign. I've named it rapid fire testing personas because we're testing out personas here. And this campaign here, as you can see is a messenger campaign.

## [03:01 - 06:05]

So it's set up as messenger. So when you click on create, you're just going to click on messages here. You're going to make sure that CBO is enabled campaign budget optimization. All right. Let me close out of this and show you guys how it's set up. So I've essentially got two personas here, persona one and persona two. And you'll see I have six different ad sets for those. The first one, I've got three different targeting options as with the second one. So I've got an introspace audience here for coaches. I've got a lookalike. I've got a broad one. Also if there's things that are more relevant to your audience, but try to keep your creative as close as possible with the exception of headlines and copy to your other persona or your other service offering, because you want to minimize the number of variables that you're taking into this test, right? The more variables you bring into this test, the less you can kind of make deductions because it could have been an image that threw the whole thing off, right? It's not uncommon that you actually get clients from rapid fire tests, but the goal is actually not that we're getting leads. It's not actually, that's not the point. The point is that we can identify the funnel or the messaging or the positioning that's

## [06:05 - 08:13]

going to get us the most traction as fast as possible. Now again, just to remind you here, this is only because it is way more effective to narrow down your marketing to speak to a specific person and a specific service offer and result than it is to go really broad. So it's way more effective to say, hey, I'm a specialist in Facebook group strategies to help you build your own audience so that you reduce the risk and the reliance that you have on Amazon or other traffic channels than it is to say, hey, I'm a Facebook ad specialist, right? One of them is really blanket and it's only going to work once you have a lot of traction in the market and once people really trust you. The other one's going to be a lot easier to get clients from because it's super specific and it takes a very specific position. Now you're going to build that one proposition out, that one prospecting using that core solution and then you just go and build another one and another one and another one. So it's not to say that that's the only service you'll ever offer to that persona ever again. It's just to say for marketing, for your prospecting, you should focus on one service offer, one persona at a time to really help you leverage what you're doing a lot better. Once you get a handle on it, then expand that out just like brands who expand out their product skews. Now when you're looking at the results for this, you're looking at messages. Ultimately double check the quality because sometimes messenger ads can have some funky traffic that comes through that. But check your cost per message against the two different personas and if you're not really getting messages, then check your soft stats like your click through rates, your cost per click to really assess whether or not you're getting any interest for what you're saying. And so this is basically how we set up a rapid fire test and so you could set this up either testing different personas or you could test this out testing different service offerings that you have with your freelancing services.
